description: Wire up the Preferences window foundation: tauri-plugin-store, native macOS menu, Cmd+, shortcut, and a separate blank Preferences webview window
acceptance_criteria: Cmd+, opens a blank Preferences window as a separate OS window
Pressing Cmd+, again when the window is already open brings it to front
The Preferences window is 520x400, not resizable, titled 'Preferences'
tauri-plugin-store is registered and accessible from the frontend
The macOS app menu shows 'AI Agent Workspace' with About, Preferences... (⌘,), and Quit

description: Build the Preferences form with External Tools section: Editor, Diff Tool, and Terminal preset dropdowns with Custom input and debounced auto-save to the store
acceptance_criteria: Preferences window shows an 'External Tools' section with three labeled dropdowns
Each preset dropdown lists the correct presets with correct bundle names and a 'Custom...' option
Selecting 'Custom...' reveals an inline text input for entering an arbitrary application name
Dropdown selections and custom text changes are saved to the store with debounced writes
All fields default to unconfigured when the store is empty or fails to load
Preferences persist across app restarts
Store read and write failures show an error toast

description: Build the toast notification system: context provider, fixed-position component, auto-dismiss, stacking, and action buttons
acceptance_criteria: Triggering a toast shows a fixed-position banner in the bottom-right of the app window
Toasts auto-dismiss after 4 seconds
Up to 3 toasts stack vertically; when a 4th arrives, the oldest is dismissed
Toasts accept an optional action button; clicking it fires the provided callback
Info toasts and error toasts render with distinct visual styling
A manual close button on each toast dismisses it immediately

description: Add the session row context menu scaffold: right-click handler, six-item menu rendering with scroll-aware positioning, and dismiss via click-outside and Escape
acceptance_criteria: Right-clicking any session row shows a context menu at the cursor position
The menu contains all six items in order: Open in Finder, Open in Editor, Open in External Diff, Open in Terminal, Copy SessionID, Copy Session Path
The menu position accounts for sidebar scroll offset
Clicking outside the menu or pressing Escape dismisses it
The existing inline Rename and Delete buttons remain unchanged on the session row
The context menu shows the same six items regardless of whether the session is running, paused, or missing

description: Wire Open in Finder and clipboard copy actions to the context menu with error handling
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Open in Finder' reveals the session's workingDirectory in macOS Finder
For missing sessions, Finder reveals the first existing parent directory
Clicking 'Copy SessionID' copies the session UUID to the system clipboard
Clicking 'Copy Session Path' copies the workingDirectory to the system clipboard
Clipboard write failures show an error toast
Selecting any of these three actions dismisses the context menu

description: Build the tool launcher function and wire Open in Editor, Open in External Diff, and Open in Terminal actions to the context menu, Preferences store, toasts, and opener plugin
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Open in Editor' launches the configured External Editor in the workingDirectory
Clicking 'Open in External Diff' launches the configured External Diff Tool in the workingDirectory
Clicking 'Open in Terminal' launches the configured External Terminal in the workingDirectory
If a tool preference is unconfigured, an info toast appears with a 'Configure' button that opens the Preferences window
If an external tool launch fails, an error toast appears with the tool name
If the workingDirectory is not a git repository, the diff tool action shows a warning toast before launching the tool
Selecting any of these three actions dismisses the context menu
